pullRequest.title: [DOM-75072][DOM-74981] Refactor patch Extensions API for persister to handle patch logic

nodes.body:
i dont think we need this check.

can you explain a bit about it's purpose?

This is to verify that resulting mount config from the patch does not include both allProjects=true and mount points. I add this to mainly to guard against client error as setting allProjects and having mount points is usually not intended. However, I can also see how it's convenient to just set allProjects to true without needing to turn off all the mount points. I can remove these checks for both create and patch.
ok yeah, i understand the concern but i think since it doesnt actually affect "correctness", best at this point to remove the code -- less to maintain, less complexity

i dont think we actually need to explicitly prevent this

everything still works if there are mount points and allProjects=true
can you take out "by project id"? its true the upsert key includes project id for the two types of mount points we have now, but may not be the case for all types soon
can you take out "by project id"? its true the upsert key includes project id for the two types of mount points we have now, but may not be the case for all types soon

i checked the query, and it really is a true patch -- that's great

so i think this is a little bit confusing. calling out "If `uiMountPointTypeConfigs` is provided, top-level config fields are updated" makes it seems different, but really it is the same -- if fields in there are provided, they are patched, but if None, then nothing happens.

so how about this:
"Partially update an Extension. Absent fields are left unchanged. Any mount points within any `uiMountPointTypeConfig` are upserted."

same comment for the other description in these docs
Yes I agree, I think that's more clear!
